title: feature: Podman image set the temporary load directory
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

Set the temporary load directory

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Thanks for contributing, @cubxxw!
> 
> Are you aware of `image_copy_tmp_dir` in `man containers.conf`? You may also override it via the `TMPDIR` environment variable.
> 
> Would that work for your use case?

oh, sorry, because I happened to see podman some buildah has necessary features such as setting temporary loading directory, I think it is necessary, so I have this RP, but I don't understand what you mean

> oh, sorry, because I happened to see podman some buildah has necessary features such as setting temporary loading directory, I think it is necessary, so I have this RP, but I don't understand what you mean

What I mean is that this functionality already exists, for instance, when you set `$ TMPDIR=/my/tmpdir podman load` or the field in containers.conf above.  Would that work for you or do you need a flag?

> > oh, sorry, because I happened to see podman some buildah has necessary features such as setting temporary loading directory, I think it is necessary, so I have this RP, but I don't understand what you mean
> 
> What I mean is that this functionality already exists, for instance, when you set `$ TMPDIR=/my/tmpdir podman load` or the field in containers.conf above. Would that work for you or do you need a flag?

Yes, I need a flag, can you provide it? If not, I will close this PR

Can you elaborate why the env variable or the containers.conf config file don't work in your scenario?

I am a bit reluctant to add a flag because no other command exposes such a flag.  I am afraid that we would need to add a `--tmp*` flag to pull/push/create/... and all the other commands who may be loading/pulling an image.
Ok, I'll go back to an issue and describe it
